title: orchis latifolia subsp. impudica
intro: Orchis latifolia subsp. impudica is a sub-species of the orchid family, specifically a type of wild orchid that is native to certain regions of Europe. It is also known as the lady's fingers or wild orchis, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and calm the body. In herbal preparations, Orchis latifolia subsp. impudica is often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
